questions.stem:
—______ you ______ your homework yet?
—Yes, I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。问句中的'yet'（还，尚）是现在完成时的标志词，表示动作是否已经发生，因此问句用'Have...finished'；答句中的'an hour ago'（一小时前）是明确的过去时间状语，只能用一般过去时'finished'。A选项第一空错误，yet不与一般过去时连用；C选项第三空错误，ago不与现在完成时连用；D选项两空都错。口诀：yet/already/ever/never用现完，yesterday/ago/last night用过去。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is standing under the tree is my cousin who just came back from Shenzhen.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词是'the boy'（指人），且关系代词在从句中作主语（is standing），因此用who。which指物；whom指人但只能作宾语；whose表示'……的'，作定语。判断技巧：指人用who/whom/that，指物用which/that；从句缺主语用who/which，缺宾语用whom/which/省略。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The new environmental protection book ______ into 10 languages since it came out three years ago.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时的被动语态。时间状语'since it came out three years ago'（自从三年前出版以来）是现在完成时的标志；主语'book'与动词'translate'是被动关系（书被翻译），因此用现在完成时的被动语态'has been translated'。A是主动语态一般现在时；B是主动语态一般过去时；D是一般现在时被动语态，不能和since连用。解题关键：看到since想到现在完成时，看到'书被翻译'想到被动语态。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: Tom is ______ than any other student in his class. He is the best basketball player.
questions.explanation: 本题考查形容词比较级。句中有'than'（比），这是比较级的标志词，因此用taller。A是原级；C和D是最高级，用于三者或以上比较（常与in/of短语连用）。句型'比较级+than+any other+名词单数'表示'比其他任何……都……'，表达最高级含义。技巧：看到than就选比较级（-er/more）。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The old wooden bridge is not strong enough to support heavy trucks. It may ______ if too many trucks cross it at the same time.
questions.explanation: 本题考查语境猜词。前句说桥不够坚固，无法承受重型卡车，因此推断后句意思是'如果太多卡车同时通过，它可能会倒塌'。fall down意为'倒塌，落下'；break down意为'出故障，分解'（常用于机器、车辆）；turn down意为'调低，拒绝'；slow down意为'减速'。根据上下文'not strong enough'和'bridge'的语境，只有fall down符合逻辑。技巧：通过上下文关键词（strong/support）推断词义。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should ______ our small differences and work together to protect the environment.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词短语辨析。set aside意为'把……放一边，暂不考虑，搁置'；put away意为'把……收起来，储存'；take off意为'起飞，脱掉，突然成功'；give out意为'分发，耗尽，公布'。根据语境'我们应该搁置小分歧，共同努力保护环境'，set aside最符合句意。技巧：set aside differences是固定搭配，意为'搁置分歧'，常用于合作、谈判语境。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you don't know the meaning of a new word, you can ______ it in the dictionary.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词短语辨析。look up意为'查阅（词典、资料）'；look for意为'寻找'；look at意为'看'；look after意为'照顾'。根据语境'在词典中查单词'，应选look up。技巧：look up+word+in the dictionary是固定搭配，注意代词要放中间（look it up），名词可放中间或后面（look the word up/look up the word）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 本题考查语境推理。前文提到'plastic bottle recycling'（塑料瓶回收）和'special bins'（专用垃圾桶），后文说'They just threw bottles anywhere'（他们到处乱扔瓶子），说明学生们没有正确'分类'。sort意为'分类，整理'；use使用；collect收集；make制造。根据回收语境，sort...correctly（正确分类）最符合逻辑。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
A Green School Project

Last month, our school started an environmental project called "Green Campus". The aim was to reduce waste and teach students about recycling.

Our class decided to work on plastic bottle recycling. First, we placed special bins in every classroom. At first, many students didn't care about (1)______ them correctly. They just threw bottles anywhere.

My friend Li Wei came up with a great (2)______. He suggested putting up posters showing how long plastic takes to break down — 450 years! This shocked everyone.

After that, students started paying more attention. Every Friday afternoon, we collect the bottles and (3)______ them to the recycling center. So far, we (4)______ over 500 kilograms of plastic.

The project has changed our school a lot. Not only has it made the campus cleaner, but it has also raised our environmental (5)______. We now understand that small actions can make a big difference.

questions.explanation: 本题考查名词辨析及固定搭配。come up with an idea是固定搭配，意为'想出一个主意'。idea主意，想法；speech演讲；lesson课程，教训；article文章。后文'He suggested...'（他建议……）说明这是一个'主意/想法'，因此选idea。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
A Green School Project

Last month, our school started an environmental project called "Green Campus". The aim was to reduce waste and teach students about recycling.

Our class decided to work on plastic bottle recycling. First, we placed special bins in every classroom. At first, many students didn't care about (1)______ them correctly. They just threw bottles anywhere.

My friend Li Wei came up with a great (2)______. He suggested putting up posters showing how long plastic takes to break down — 450 years! This shocked everyone.

After that, students started paying more attention. Every Friday afternoon, we collect the bottles and (3)______ them to the recycling center. So far, we (4)______ over 500 kilograms of plastic.

The project has changed our school a lot. Not only has it made the campus cleaner, but it has also raised our environmental (5)______. We now understand that small actions can make a big difference.

questions.explanation: 本题考查动词辨析及语境理解。send...to...意为'把……送到……'；donate捐赠（通常搭配to charity）；sell卖；show展示。根据语境'我们把瓶子送到回收中心'，send最符合逻辑，表示运送的动作。donate虽然语义相关，但通常指直接捐赠给需要的人或机构，而回收中心是处理站，用send更准确。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
A Green School Project

Last month, our school started an environmental project called "Green Campus". The aim was to reduce waste and teach students about recycling.

Our class decided to work on plastic bottle recycling. First, we placed special bins in every classroom. At first, many students didn't care about (1)______ them correctly. They just threw bottles anywhere.

My friend Li Wei came up with a great (2)______. He suggested putting up posters showing how long plastic takes to break down — 450 years! This shocked everyone.

After that, students started paying more attention. Every Friday afternoon, we collect the bottles and (3)______ them to the recycling center. So far, we (4)______ over 500 kilograms of plastic.

The project has changed our school a lot. Not only has it made the campus cleaner, but it has also raised our environmental (5)______. We now understand that small actions can make a big difference.

questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时。时间状语'So far'（到目前为止）是现在完成时的标志词，表示从过去某时持续到现在的情况，因此用have recycled。A是一般过去时；C是过去完成时（用于过去的过去）；D是现在进行时。技巧：so far/up to now/by now/in the past/last few years都是现在完成时的标志词。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
A Green School Project

Last month, our school started an environmental project called "Green Campus". The aim was to reduce waste and teach students about recycling.

Our class decided to work on plastic bottle recycling. First, we placed special bins in every classroom. At first, many students didn't care about (1)______ them correctly. They just threw bottles anywhere.

My friend Li Wei came up with a great (2)______. He suggested putting up posters showing how long plastic takes to break down — 450 years! This shocked everyone.

After that, students started paying more attention. Every Friday afternoon, we collect the bottles and (3)______ them to the recycling center. So far, we (4)______ over 500 kilograms of plastic.

The project has changed our school a lot. Not only has it made the campus cleaner, but it has also raised our environmental (5)______. We now understand that small actions can make a big difference.

questions.explanation: 本题考查名词辨析及语境理解。awareness意为'意识'，environmental awareness即'环保意识'；protection保护；pollution污染；development发展。前文提到项目让校园更干净，后文说'我们明白了小行动能带来大改变'，说明提高了'意识'。raise awareness是固定搭配，意为'提高意识'。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
A Green School Project

Last month, our school started an environmental project called "Green Campus". The aim was to reduce waste and teach students about recycling.

Our class decided to work on plastic bottle recycling. First, we placed special bins in every classroom. At first, many students didn't care about (1)______ them correctly. They just threw bottles anywhere.

My friend Li Wei came up with a great (2)______. He suggested putting up posters showing how long plastic takes to break down — 450 years! This shocked everyone.

After that, students started paying more attention. Every Friday afternoon, we collect the bottles and (3)______ them to the recycling center. So far, we (4)______ over 500 kilograms of plastic.

The project has changed our school a lot. Not only has it made the campus cleaner, but it has also raised our environmental (5)______. We now understand that small actions can make a big difference.

questions.stem: According to the study mentioned in Paragraph 1, how many hours do teenagers spend on screens daily on average?
questions.explanation: 本题是细节理解题。根据第一段第一句'A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7.5 hours on screens every day.'（最近的一项研究显示，青少年平均每天花7.5小时在屏幕上），直接找到答案7.5 hours。A选项6小时是第三段提到的社交媒体使用时间；C选项20分钟是专家建议的休息规则；D选项450年是塑料分解时间（干扰项来自完形填空，注意区分不同文章信息）。技巧：细节题要准确定位原文，注意数字对应。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Screen Time and Teen Health

A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7.5 hours on screens every day. This includes smartphones, computers, and televisions. While technology helps with learning and communication, excessive screen time can cause health problems.

Dr. Chen, a health researcher, explains that looking at screens for too long leads to eye strain and poor sleep. "The blue light from screens tricks the brain into thinking it's still daytime," says Dr. Chen. This makes it hard for teens to fall asleep at night.

Besides physical health, screen time also affects mental health. The study found that teens who spend more than 6 hours daily on social media are more likely to feel anxious or depressed. They often compare themselves with others online, which lowers their self-esteem.

However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational apps and video calls with family can be beneficial. The key is balance. Experts recommend the "20-20-20 rule": every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Parents can help by setting screen-free zones at home, such as during meals and before bedtime. Schools are also taking action. Some have started "phone-free days" to encourage face-to-face communication.

Remember, technology is a tool. It's up to us to use it wisely.

questions.stem: What does the underlined word 'excessive' in Paragraph 1 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 本题是词义猜测题。根据上下文，前句说每天7.5小时，后句说'会导致健康问题'，因此excessive应该是'过多的，过量的'意思。limited有限的；too much太多；necessary必要的；proper适当的。技巧：通过因果逻辑判断，'导致健康问题'通常是因为'过多'而非'适当'或'有限'。前缀ex-表示'出，外'，cess表示'走'，excessive即'走出界限的'→过度的。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Screen Time and Teen Health

A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7.5 hours on screens every day. This includes smartphones, computers, and televisions. While technology helps with learning and communication, excessive screen time can cause health problems.

Dr. Chen, a health researcher, explains that looking at screens for too long leads to eye strain and poor sleep. "The blue light from screens tricks the brain into thinking it's still daytime," says Dr. Chen. This makes it hard for teens to fall asleep at night.

Besides physical health, screen time also affects mental health. The study found that teens who spend more than 6 hours daily on social media are more likely to feel anxious or depressed. They often compare themselves with others online, which lowers their self-esteem.

However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational apps and video calls with family can be beneficial. The key is balance. Experts recommend the "20-20-20 rule": every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Parents can help by setting screen-free zones at home, such as during meals and before bedtime. Schools are also taking action. Some have started "phone-free days" to encourage face-to-face communication.

Remember, technology is a tool. It's up to us to use it wisely.

questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage?
options: A. All screen time is bad for teenagers and should be avoided.
B. Teenagers should never use social media because it causes depression.
C. Using technology wisely with balance is important for teen health.
D. Parents should forbid their children from using smartphones completely.
questions.explanation: 本题是推理判断题。A选项说所有屏幕时间都有害，与第四段'However, not all screen time is harmful'矛盾；B选项说绝不能用社交媒体，过于绝对，文中只说超过6小时可能焦虑；C选项'明智地平衡使用科技对青少年健康很重要'符合第四段'The key is balance'和最后一段'It's up to us to use it wisely'；D选项说父母应完全禁止，与文中'setting screen-free zones'（设置无屏幕区域，而非完全禁止）不符。技巧：推理题要基于原文但不停留在表面，注意绝对化词汇（all/never/completely）通常是错的。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Screen Time and Teen Health

A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7.5 hours on screens every day. This includes smartphones, computers, and televisions. While technology helps with learning and communication, excessive screen time can cause health problems.

Dr. Chen, a health researcher, explains that looking at screens for too long leads to eye strain and poor sleep. "The blue light from screens tricks the brain into thinking it's still daytime," says Dr. Chen. This makes it hard for teens to fall asleep at night.

Besides physical health, screen time also affects mental health. The study found that teens who spend more than 6 hours daily on social media are more likely to feel anxious or depressed. They often compare themselves with others online, which lowers their self-esteem.

However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational apps and video calls with family can be beneficial. The key is balance. Experts recommend the "20-20-20 rule": every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Parents can help by setting screen-free zones at home, such as during meals and before bedtime. Schools are also taking action. Some have started "phone-free days" to encourage face-to-face communication.

Remember, technology is a tool. It's up to us to use it wisely.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: B. How to Make Friends Online
C. Screen Time and Teen Health
questions.explanation: 本题是主旨大意题。文章第一段讲屏幕使用时间，第二段讲身体健康（眼睛、睡眠），第三段讲心理健康，第四段讲如何平衡使用，整体围绕'屏幕时间与青少年健康'展开。A选项智能手机历史，文中未提；B选项如何网上交友，只在第三段提及社交媒体的负面影响；D选项教育应用，只在第四段作为正面例子提到。C选项涵盖了全文主题。技巧：最佳标题要覆盖全文主要内容，既不能太宽泛也不能太片面，通常首段或尾段会点明主题。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Screen Time and Teen Health

A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7.5 hours on screens every day. This includes smartphones, computers, and televisions. While technology helps with learning and communication, excessive screen time can cause health problems.

Dr. Chen, a health researcher, explains that looking at screens for too long leads to eye strain and poor sleep. "The blue light from screens tricks the brain into thinking it's still daytime," says Dr. Chen. This makes it hard for teens to fall asleep at night.

Besides physical health, screen time also affects mental health. The study found that teens who spend more than 6 hours daily on social media are more likely to feel anxious or depressed. They often compare themselves with others online, which lowers their self-esteem.

However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational apps and video calls with family can be beneficial. The key is balance. Experts recommend the "20-20-20 rule": every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Parents can help by setting screen-free zones at home, such as during meals and before bedtime. Schools are also taking action. Some have started "phone-free days" to encourage face-to-face communication.

Remember, technology is a tool. It's up to us to use it wisely.

options: A. Then, prioritize your tasks and stick to your plan.
B. However, with good time management, you can do both well.
C. As a result, many students give up their favorite activities.
D. Even short periods can bring you joy and relaxation.
E. On the other hand, studying all day without breaks is harmful.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接与逻辑关系。前文说'许多学生因为繁重的学业而难以找到时间从事爱好'，后文说'以下是帮助你实现这种平衡的建议'，因此空格处需要一个转折句，承上启下，引出'平衡'的概念。B选项'然而，通过良好的时间管理，你可以把两者都做好'符合逻辑。A选项是具体建议，应在后面；C选项是结果，但后文是解决方案，衔接不当；D选项讲短时间，与后文'tips'衔接不自然；E选项讲休息的重要性，与后文建议列表衔接不如B自然。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Balancing Study and Hobbies

Many students struggle to find time for their hobbies because of heavy schoolwork. (17)______ Here are some tips to help you achieve this balance.

First, make a schedule. Write down all your homework and activities in a planner. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important tasks or waste time deciding what to do next.

Second, use small pockets of time. You don't need hours to enjoy a hobby. (19)______ For example, you can read a book for 15 minutes before bed or draw during lunch break.

Finally, don't forget to rest. (20)______ If you feel too stressed, take a short walk or listen to music. A relaxed mind helps you study better.

Remember, hobbies are not a waste of time. They help you relax and develop new skills.

options: A. Then, prioritize your tasks and stick to your plan.
B. However, with good time management, you can do both well.
C. As a result, many students give up their favorite activities.
D. Even short periods can bring you joy and relaxation.
E. On the other hand, studying all day without breaks is harmful.
questions.explanation: 本题考查指代衔接与逻辑顺序。前文说'首先，制定时间表，在计划本上写下所有作业和活动'，后文说'这样你就不会忘记重要任务'，因此空格处应该是制定计划后的下一步行动。A选项'然后，确定任务的优先级并坚持执行你的计划'承接上文，Then表示顺序，且'your plan'指代上文的'schedule'。其他选项：B是转折，C是结果，D讲短时间，E讲休息，都与'制定计划'的语境不符。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Balancing Study and Hobbies

Many students struggle to find time for their hobbies because of heavy schoolwork. (17)______ Here are some tips to help you achieve this balance.

First, make a schedule. Write down all your homework and activities in a planner. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important tasks or waste time deciding what to do next.

Second, use small pockets of time. You don't need hours to enjoy a hobby. (19)______ For example, you can read a book for 15 minutes before bed or draw during lunch break.

Finally, don't forget to rest. (20)______ If you feel too stressed, take a short walk or listen to music. A relaxed mind helps you study better.

Remember, hobbies are not a waste of time. They help you relax and develop new skills.

options: A. Then, prioritize your tasks and stick to your plan.
B. However, with good time management, you can do both well.
C. As a result, many students give up their favorite activities.
D. Even short periods can bring you joy and relaxation.
E. On the other hand, studying all day without breaks is harmful.
questions.explanation: 本题考查语境衔接。前文说'你不需要花好几个小时来享受爱好'，后文举例'你可以睡前读15分钟书或午餐时画画'，因此空格处应该强调'短时间'的价值。D选项'即使是短时间段也能带给你快乐和放松'承上启下，short periods对应前文small pockets of time和hours，也对应后文的15 minutes。其他选项与'small pockets of time'主题不符。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Balancing Study and Hobbies

Many students struggle to find time for their hobbies because of heavy schoolwork. (17)______ Here are some tips to help you achieve this balance.

First, make a schedule. Write down all your homework and activities in a planner. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important tasks or waste time deciding what to do next.

Second, use small pockets of time. You don't need hours to enjoy a hobby. (19)______ For example, you can read a book for 15 minutes before bed or draw during lunch break.

Finally, don't forget to rest. (20)______ If you feel too stressed, take a short walk or listen to music. A relaxed mind helps you study better.

Remember, hobbies are not a waste of time. They help you relax and develop new skills.

options: A. Then, prioritize your tasks and stick to your plan.
B. However, with good time management, you can do both well.
C. As a result, many students give up their favorite activities.
D. Even short periods can bring you joy and relaxation.
E. On the other hand, studying all day without breaks is harmful.
questions.explanation: 本题考查逻辑关系与段落衔接。本段主题是'rest'（休息），前文说'最后，不要忘记休息'，后文说'如果你感到压力太大，散散步或听听音乐'，因此空格处应该解释为什么要休息，或说明不休息的坏处。E选项'另一方面，整天学习不休息是有害的'引出休息的必要性，On the other hand表示转折对比（虽然要努力学习，但……），符合逻辑。A、B、C、D都不涉及'rest'或'studying all day'的对比。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Balancing Study and Hobbies

Many students struggle to find time for their hobbies because of heavy schoolwork. (17)______ Here are some tips to help you achieve this balance.

First, make a schedule. Write down all your homework and activities in a planner. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important tasks or waste time deciding what to do next.

Second, use small pockets of time. You don't need hours to enjoy a hobby. (19)______ For example, you can read a book for 15 minutes before bed or draw during lunch break.

Finally, don't forget to rest. (20)______ If you feel too stressed, take a short walk or listen to music. A relaxed mind helps you study better.

Remember, hobbies are not a waste of time. They help you relax and develop new skills.
